title: Chapter 53
text: Sources Sources ( Figure 38 ) are also steady states. But now, nearby points move away. This is like a lump of dough perched on an overturned bowl. It can be made to balance at the top, if you're very careful, but if you give it a push, it rolls sideways and falls off. That is, the steady state is unstable. Remember that the dough is only very slightly sticky: it won't stick to a slope. And think of a bowl with a rounded bottom, not a flat one. Perhaps a better analogy is trying to balance one smooth pebble on top of another. You can do it – with care – but a breath of wind, and it slides off.
